title: clematis × secondaireana
intro: Clematis × secondaireana, also known as Clematis, is a flowering plant in the Ranunculaceae family, primarily grown for its ornamental value and medicinal properties. The plant contains flavonoids and coumarins, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Clematis include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to reduce swelling and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, Clematis is often used in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat a range of ailments.
